step_by_step_thinking:
Your final answer must be among the following:
A. Rat bite  
B. Cate bit  
C. Dog bite  
D. Monkey bite  

(Only write your selected letter)

I agree with agent1’s assessment. Monkey bites pose a significantly higher risk of rabies transmission due to the high prevalence of rabies in wild and captive non-human primates, especially in regions where these animals live freely or are kept without proper quarantine. This makes them uniquely distinct among the listed options requiring prompt initiation of post-exposure prophylaxis—even more than other types like cat or dog bites—which often involve lower-risk scenarios. Thus, the appropriate answer remains D. Monkey bite.

D
